title: Unscheduled power outages amid heatwave: Locals block NH-57 in Odisha's Ranpur - Odisha TV
full_text:
Thousands of residents from more than 10 villages staged a road blockade on National Highway 57 at Binodapoda in Ranapur in Odisha's Nayagarh district late on Saturday night, protesting against frequent and unannounced power outages in the area.
The protest reportedly took place despite government directions that the electricity supply should not be disrupted without prior information or announcement. The blockade led to major traffic disruption, with hundreds of vehicles stranded on both sides of the highway.
Protesters Enter Electricity Office
Apart from blocking the national highway, the agitating residents also entered the local electricity office premises and staged a protest against the recurring power cuts.
The situation remained tense as a large number of people gathered at the protest site, demanding a resolution to the issue of unscheduled electricity outages affecting their villages.
Traffic Affected, Officials Intervene
The road blockade severely affected vehicular movement on NH-57, resulting in long queues of vehicles on both sides of the road.
Following the protest, the Section Engineer concerned and personnel from Ranapur police station reached the spot and held discussions with the agitating residents. After the talks, the protesters withdrew the road blockade, restoring normal traffic movement on the highway.
